page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography instructions.

The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, but does not include any bundled local storage or public IPv4 addresses. It is, however, Premium Storage capable. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1896.88 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0. Multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3867.37 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3524.0. Memory read bandwidth is also weak, measuring 208.51 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 29.75 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 25.9 GB/sec.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4s_v5 offers a standard hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. It achieves average throughput in static web serving at 6.44 GB/sec and average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, it exhibits weak performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression. This virtual machine is best suited for light web serving, basic application hosting, and text processing tasks that do not require high multi-core scaling or local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include bundled local storage but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. The server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as light web serving, basic application hosting, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon processor, suitable for light web serving and basic application hosting.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon processor on Hyper-V.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine with balanced memory and compute resources for standard application hosting and light web serving.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM.
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage.
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance.
Weak multi-core scaling and database operations throughput.
Best suited for light web serving, basic application hosting, and text processing.
